body.title: Generalized agent kill switch: halt any registered agent via MCP
body.result: HaltRegistry (agent_id-keyed flat file), PolicyGate check #0, Skeleton agent_halt/agent_resume MCP tools, 18 tests
body.description:
Igor has safe mode via igor.switches.cfg. There is no generalized mechanism to halt any registered agent. Add an MCP tool (on the skeleton or policy device): agent_halt(agent_id, reason) — marks the agent's registry entry as halted, shim policy gate denies all further tool calls from that agent, agent receives a halt notification on its mailbox.

agent_resume(agent_id) clears the halt. Both actions write to the decision bill of materials (T-shim-policy-gate). Halt state survives rack restart (written to flat-file registry).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/skeleton/ (halt MCP tool), ~/.unseen_university/registry/ (halted field), unseen_university/skeleton/base_shim.py (check halted before dispatch)
**Design rules:** flat-file registry survives DB outage; halt must work even if Postgres is down
**Scope boundary:** IN — halt/resume any registered agent, shim enforcement, flat-file persistence, decision log entry; OUT — graceful drain (in-flight calls complete), cascading halt to dependent agents
**Completion criteria:** agent_halt('test-agent', 'reason') causes all subsequent tool calls from test-agent to be denied; agent_resume restores normal operation; halt state persists across rack restart
